description_long: This is a tall, cylindrical terracotta vase with a slightly tapered body and a pronounced, rounded lip at the top. The surface is matte and textured, giving it a rustic, handcrafted appearance typical of traditional pottery. A single, thick spout or pipe protrudes from the lower side of the vase, curving outward, which may serve as a drainage or decorative feature. The color is a consistent reddish-brown, reminiscent of natural clay, with subtle variations in tone that suggest hand-finishing. The vase appears hollow and capable of holding water or other contents, with a wide opening at the top. There are no visible decorative motifs or embellishments beyond the basic form and the functional spout.
description: A tall, rustic terracotta vase with a wide lip and a single curved spout near the base, finished in a matte reddish-brown clay texture.
description_view_0: Front view shows the vase's cylindrical body, wide lip, and the spout extending straight out from the lower center.
description_view_1: Angled view reveals the spout's curved shape and its attachment to the lower side of the vase.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the vase's plain, uninterrupted surface with no spout visible.
description_view_3: Side view highlights the spout's full curvature and its position relative to the vase's body.
